requirements.summary: Restore the RB (Return/Replenishment Buy) order logic so that RB-type orders are correctly handled throughout the order lifecycle, distinguishable from CO-type orders, display correctly in TotalOrdersTab grouped view, support SuppliedQtyDialog partial-supply flow, and transition through status stages without regression
acceptanceCriteria: RB-type orders appear in TotalOrdersTab and can be distinguished from CO-type orders.
Selecting RB orders and triggering supply opens the SuppliedQtyDialog, allowing partial quantities to be supplied while remaining units remain as Pending.
Fully supplied RB orders transition to Ready status correctly.
RB orders flow through Hallmark and ReturnFromHallmark status stages without errors.
CO-type order flows are unaffected by the RB logic restoration.
No regression in existing order status transitions or filtering.

file_operations.description: Restore RB order type visibility and handling in the grouped order view. Ensure RB orders are displayed alongside CO orders with appropriate visual distinction (badge or column indicator). Add or restore the 'Supply RB Orders' action button that opens SuppliedQtyDialog when RB-type orders are selected. Verify that the existing delete and mark-ready actions continue to work for both CO and RB order types. Ensure filtering and grouping logic accommodates both order types without conflict.

Ensure the dialog correctly handles RB orders by allowing users to enter supplied quantities for each selected order. Validate that partial supply logic is intact: if supplied quantity is less than total order quantity, remaining units must stay as Pending status orders. On submission, call the backend supplyOrder method for each order with the user-entered quantity. After successful submission, invalidate relevant query caches (orders, pending orders) to reflect the updated state. Confirm that the dialog closes properly after submission and displays appropriate success or error feedback.

Add an order type column or badge to visually distinguish RB orders from CO orders in the table view. Ensure that bulk actions (mark as ready, delete) work seamlessly for both order types without introducing regressions. Verify that the table correctly displays RB orders retrieved from the backend alongside CO orders, and that selection state is maintained across order types.

Ensure that RB orders that have been fully supplied and transitioned to Ready status appear correctly in the Ready tab alongside CO orders. Verify that the grouped-by-design view and multi-select functionality work for RB orders. Confirm that bulk actions (revert to Pending, export) apply to RB orders without errors. Add visual distinction (order type badge or column) if not already present.

Verify that RB orders can transition to Hallmark status and are displayed correctly in the Hallmark tab. Ensure the grouped view, search/filter by karigar and order type, and batch status update to ReturnFromHallmark work for RB orders without regression. Add order type filtering support if not already present, allowing users to filter by RB or CO. Confirm that export to JPEG/PDF includes RB orders appropriately.

Ensure the Dashboard page correctly loads and displays RB orders across all tabs (Total Orders, Ready, Hallmark). Verify that summary cards (order count, weight, quantity, CO count) account for RB orders in their calculations where appropriate. Confirm that the tabbed interface switches between views without losing RB order data or causing layout issues.

Ensure that RB orders are included in the ageing stock view and are displayed with the correct age-based color coding (green/yellow/red). Verify that bulk selection and the supplied-quantity dialog work for RB orders. Confirm that marking RB orders as ready via the supplied-quantity flow functions correctly on this page.

Ensure that RB orders assigned to a specific karigar are displayed correctly on the KarigarDetail page. Verify that selection, mark as ready, and export actions work for RB orders without regression. Add visual distinction for order type if not already present.

Review and ensure that all query hooks (e.g., useGetOrders, useGetOrdersWithMappings) correctly fetch and return RB orders alongside CO orders. Verify that mutations (e.g., useSupplyOrder, useBatchUpdateOrderStatus) handle RB orders correctly and invalidate the appropriate query caches after operations. Confirm that the supplyOrder mutation logic supports partial supply for RB orders, maintaining Pending status for unsupplied units.
